Overhang: ~1.59M Make Whole shares settle by 2027-03-15 plus evergreen 2026 Plan/ESPP/Inducement reserves.

dossiers.bear_case: As of spin time: a leveraged pure-play on the most brutally cyclical commodity in semis. The same document showing a profitable Q1 FY25 shows a pro forma FY24 net loss of $(856)M on $6,663M revenue — one year earlier the standalone entity would have been burning cash against $172M of pro forma annual interest expense. Off-balance-sheet exposure dwarfed the funded debt: $5,643M of Flash Ventures commitments ($2,281M due within nine months) plus $1,299M (¥208B) of JV lease guarantees subject to acceleration on cancellation events — total obligations ~4x the funded debt, owed into JVs SNDK jointly controls but does not control outright (Kioxia relationship risk is a named risk factor). Spinco paid WDC $920M cash plus all debt proceeds as consideration, so the $2.0B borrowing existed to fund the parent, not the business. Incentive paper was weak: all equity was converted WDC awards; PSUs converted to time-based-only vesting (performance linkage deleted); VP+ employees kept BOTH WDC and Spinco awards; a $39M cash (not equity) transaction bonus; directors and officers collectively under 1%. And the behavioral tell never fired: zero open-market insider buys from distribution through 2026-07, and $12.6M of insider selling into the run. [OUTCOME: the bear case lost — but only because flash pricing exploded; the document-level risks (JV commitments, cyclicality, weak alignment) were real and would have capped position size.]
dossiers.bull_case: As of spin time, from the information statement alone: this was the opposite of a garbage barge — WDC kept the declining HDD business and spun out the flash franchise with the crown-jewel assets (49.9% of Flash Ventures JVs with Kioxia across 8 Japanese fabs, the SanDisk consumer brand, 144M shares against $11.7B pro forma APIC). Balance sheet was deliberately conservative: $1,339M retained cash vs $1,943M long-term debt (~$600M net debt on $13.4B total capitalization) — light leverage for a company that just printed pro forma Q1 FY25 EPS of $1.19 ($1,883M revenue, $172M net income) at the start of a flash-price upcycle. Annualizing that quarter put the $46.50 trigger price near 10x earnings with the cycle inflecting. The forced-seller setup was textbook: 1/3 distribution ratio created odd-lot positions, Vanguard (8.9%) and BlackRock (6.4%) inherited shares tracking a parent index, and WDC's 19.9% stake had a disclosed 12-month disposal clock via debt-for-equity exchanges. Parent CEO David Goeckeler chose to run the spinco — the classic Greenblatt tell of where management thought the value was. [OUTCOME: The thesis played out beyond any base rate — the memory supercycle took SNDK from the $46.50 trigger (2025-03-06) to ~$2,032 by 2026-07-03, roughly 43.7x, with NO stock split (XBRL shares outstanding 144.9M at Feb 2025 → 148.1M at Apr 2026; insider Form 4 sale prices trace the climb: $195 Dec-2025, $628 Feb-2026, $1,426 May-2026, $2,088 Jul-2026). The system graduated it LATE. Note for calibration: the gates as scored here would have said WATCH, not OWN — the incentive gate (0.3, converted awards only, zero open-market buys ever) correctly described weak insider alignment yet the stock 43x'd anyway, because the driver was the commodity cycle, which no Form 10 gate measures.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: (1) WDC itself: retained 19.9% (28,626,330 shares) with a disclosed plan to dispose of ALL of it within 12 months via debt-for-equity exchanges with WDC creditors and/or further distributions — a structured, calendar-bound seller whose exchanged blocks land with credit desks that do not want equity. (2) Index-mandate mismatch: distribution came from an S&P 500 parent; Vanguard (8.9%) and BlackRock (6.4%) inherited stakes via WDC positions, with mechanical rebalancing pressure on any index reclassification of the smaller spinco. (3) Odd-lot/ratio friction: 1/3-share distribution ratio meant a 100-share WDC holder received ~33 SNDK shares (~$1,500 at trigger), classic sub-scale positions sold indiscriminately. (4) When-issued to regular-way transition liquidity air pocket in the first weeks (trigger fired 2025-03-06, 9 trading days post-distribution). WDC's proxy arrangement (voting retained shares proportionally) confirmed the stake was inert inventory, not strategic.
dossiers.key_risk: Flash-price cyclicality compounding fixed JV obligations: $5,643M of Flash Ventures commitments and $1,299M of lease guarantees (with acceleration triggers) against $1,943M funded debt, in a business that pro forma lost $(856)M in FY24 — a downturn year would have forced funding of Kioxia JV fabs from a shrinking cash pile.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 sweep (CIK 0002023554, distribution 2025-02-21 through 2026-07-03, 113 post-distribution transactions): ZERO open-market purchases (code P) by any insider at any point — including the first weeks of trading at $35-50 when the behavioral tell would have fired. 5 insiders sold $12,610,676 across 9 code-S transactions, all into the rally: Sayiner 1,271 sh @$195 (2025-12-03) and 579 sh @$1,503 (2026-05-08); Suzuki 3,500 sh @$628 (2026-02-25); Pokorny 2,446 sh @$1,426 (2026-05-12); Ilkbahar 2,000 sh @~$1,756 (2026-06-01); Shek 1,200 sh @$1,736-2,088 (Jun-Jul 2026). Not an FPI — channel was fully lit; the absence of buys is evidence, not a blind spot. Balance of activity was award conversions/grants per the Employee Matters Agreement. Sale prices independently corroborate the no-split price path.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): Pro forma as of 2024-09-27 (Capitalization + Unaudited Pro Forma sections): cash $1,339M; long-term debt $1,943M (from ~$2,000M principal — term loan + notes — net of $30M OID and $27M issuance costs, incl. $20M current); net debt ~$604M; APIC $11,691M; total capitalization $13,387M; ~144M shares out (346M WDC shares x 1/3 ratio). Consideration to parent: $920M cash payment to WDC plus all remaining debt-financing net proceeds; $296M notes-due-to-Parent settled at spin; $39M cash transaction bonuses to Spinco employees. Pro forma P&L: Q1 FY25 (3mo ended 2024-09-27) revenue $1,883M, operating income $283M, net income $172M, diluted EPS $1.19; FY24 (ended 2024-06-28) revenue $6,663M, operating loss $(562)M, net loss $(856)M, $(5.94)/sh — the two periods bracket the flash cycle trough. Off-balance-sheet: Flash Ventures commitments $5,643M ($2,281M due remaining 9mo of FY25) + operating leases $295M + purchase obligations $276M = $6,214M; separately $1,299M (¥208B) Flash Ventures lease guarantees with covenant/cancellation acceleration risk. Structure: 80.1% distributed, 19.9% retained by WDC for <=12mo disposal; tax-free to holders; Nasdaq listing SNDK. SPLIT VERIFICATION: dei:EntityCommonStockSharesOutstanding 144,863,251 (2025-02-27) -> 148,089,758 (2026-04-24) — no split occurred; the ~$2,032 current price is a raw price, ~43.7x the $46.50 trigger, implying ~$300B market cap. Do not quote spin-time multiples against today's price without noting this.

dossiers.bear_case: AS OF SPIN (document alone): this is an externally-managed-vehicle structure with the governance features that make such vehicles trade at a discount forever — the fee is payable regardless of CompoSecure shareholder outcomes, the agreement expressly disclaims fiduciary duty and waives the corporate-opportunity doctrine ("Additional Activities of Resolute Holdings"), and the Risk Factors admit the fee formula "may lead us to place undue emphasis on the maximization of such measures at the expense of other criteria, such as preservation of a managed company's capital." Revenue is 100% one client; the CompoSecure Management Agreement can be killed without a Termination Fee on Kick-Out Events, and the fee drops to zero in any period Adjusted EBITDA is <= 0. Key-man risk on Cote and Knott is named explicitly. Dilution machinery is pre-loaded: the Omnibus Plan reserves 1,272,962 shares (15% of the 8.49M outstanding) plus a 5% annual evergreen through 2035. The 50.2% insider block means minority holders have no vote that matters. And at the fire price ($35.23, ~$299M cap) the shell was already ~22x its ~$13.5M run-rate fee revenue — the document supports quality, not statistical cheapness. Executive sign-on equity ($6M options for Cote, $6M for Knott, $5.5M RSUs for Schoen) is all CompoSecure stock, not spinco stock — the weakest tier of paper alignment. [OUTCOME: none of these broke the trade within the horizon; the single-client concentration and EMV-governance discount were overwhelmed by the perpetuity-contract math and the founder ownership block.]
dossiers.bull_case: AS OF SPIN (document alone): the Form 10 describes a debt-free, six-employee, asset-light ManCo whose sole asset is a quasi-unbreakable contract — a quarterly fee of 2.5% of CompoSecure Holdings' trailing-12-month Adjusted EBITDA (~$135M TTM at Q3'24 per the pro forma note (h) table, so a ~$13.5M/yr run-rate), on a 10-year term that AUTO-RENEWS for successive 10-year terms, protected by a Termination Fee equal to the GREATEST of the fair market value or NPV of the fee stream "in perpetuity" or 4x trailing 24-month fees (Certain Relationships — Management Fee / Termination Fee). The alignment fact pattern is exceptional for a spinco: Resolute Compo Holdings (the Tungsten/John Cote/Knott vehicle that bought Brookfield's CompoSecure stake for cash per the Nov 2024 13D/A cited in footnote 3) receives 48.4% of RHLD pro-rata; directors and officers as a group hold 50.2% (Security Ownership table: 4,261,655 of 8,486,415 shares). David Cote's Honeywell record is the marketing, but the structure is the substance: platform optionality to bolt additional managed companies onto a fixed six-person cost base, plus the Letter Agreement delegating CompoSecure equity-issuance authority for M&A — the spinco controls the parent's acquisition engine. [OUTCOME: this is the read history graded correct — RHLD dumped -47% to $24.91 on flowback, Nemesis fired 2025-03-20 at $35.23, and the stock reached $143.66 (+308%) as the market repriced the perpetual fee stream and the Cote platform narrative; insiders later bought $2.1M more in the open market at $108-171.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: Textbook maximum-flowback setup, all disclosed in the document: (1) 1-for-12 distribution ratio produced only 8,486,415 shares — a micro-cap sliver spun to large-cap-fintech holders; (2) ~50.2% locked with insiders leaves an effective float of ~4.2M shares, so modest selling moves price violently; (3) the spin was TAXABLE (cover page), and the Risk Factors explicitly predict holders selling "to cover anticipated taxes"; (4) no index inheritance — Risk Factors: "in the case of index funds, if we are not a participant in the index in which they are investing" they will sell; (5) profile mismatch — holders owned a metal-payment-card manufacturer and received a six-person external asset manager: "our business profile and market capitalization may not fit the investment objectives of some CompoSecure stockholders"; (6) EGC + smaller-reporting-company status suppresses institutional coverage. The board itself acknowledged "the risk of volatility in our stock price immediately following the Spin-Off due to sales by CompoSecure stockholders whose investment objectives may no longer be met." [OUTCOME: exactly this happened — -47% to $24.91 within three weeks.]
dossiers.key_risk: Single-contract dependence: every dollar of revenue comes from one managed company, and CompoSecure Holdings can terminate without paying the perpetuity Termination Fee on Kick-Out Events (felony/securities-law judgment, fraud, bankruptcy of Resolute), while the fee itself goes to zero in any period CompoSecure Adjusted EBITDA is non-positive (Certain Relationships — Management Fee; Risk Factors: "The termination of the CompoSecure Management Agreement... would materially and adversely affect us").
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Not an FPI — domestic filer, Form 4 channel fully lit. Sweep since distribution (2025-02-28, run 2026-07-03): 31 transactions; 17 open-market purchases (code P) by 4 insiders totaling $2,098,154 — Timothy Mahoney (director, ~$1.50M at $152-171, Nov 2025), CFO Kurt Schoen (~$419K across Nov 2025 at $165/$149, Mar 2026 at $142, May 2026 at $108), John Cote (~$79K, Mar 2026 at $142-145), Krishna Mikkilineni (~$100K, May 2026 at $108.52). One sale: director Jane Thompson, $230K on 2026-03-17. CRITICAL TIMING CAVEAT for gate calibration: ZERO open-market buys occurred inside the fast-tier fire window — the only Form 4s at spin were code A (pro-rata distribution/awards, incl. Knott's and John Cote's 4,107,534-share Resolute Compo block) and code J housekeeping. All cash buying came Nov 2025 onward, AFTER the rerating, at prices 3-5x the fire price. So the as-of-spin incentive score had to be earned from the ownership table (48.4% cash-purchased founder block), not from observed buys; the later buying corroborates the alignment read but was not available evidence.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): scores are AS OF SPIN TIME from the 10-12B/A information statement alone; the known +308% outcome appears only in prose. Pro forma facts: Resolute Holdings was formed 2024-09-27 and "has had no activity since formation" — a clean-sheet shell, not a liability dump. Standalone capitalization: $10M working-capital funding from CompoSecure Holdings (pro forma note (a)) and "We expect to have no debt outstanding immediately following the Spin-Off" (Business — Competitive Strengths). ~6 employees, leased NYC office, no owned real property. Revenue engine: quarterly fee = 2.5% of CompoSecure Holdings' TTM Management Agreement Adjusted EBITDA, paid in arrears in cash; the note (h) table shows TTM Q3'24 Adjusted EBITDA of ~$135M (quarters 34.4/34.8/33.4/32.7), implying ~$3.4M/quarter, ~$13.5M/yr run-rate — roughly 10% of the managed company's EBITDA annually, forever. Accounting wrinkle: under ASC 810 RHLD must CONSOLIDATE CompoSecure Holdings' full financials (pro forma basis-of-presentation note), so consolidated statements show CompoSecure's inventory, receivables, and credit-facility debt — none of it recourse to the standalone six-person spinco; screeners reading the consolidated balance sheet would wrongly see a leveraged manufacturer. 8,486,415 shares outstanding at 1:12 off 101,836,981 CMPO shares. Tax adjustments modeled at 22% federal + 6.5% state.

dossiers.bear_case: [OUTCOME: This was the big-cap dud. Spun 2025-06-23, shallow -10% dump, Nemesis fired 2025-07-07 at $50.54; twelve months later $52.73, +4.3% — no re-rating. The gates as scored here would have kept it at watch, and the binding constraint was neglect: at ~$27.9B market cap (552,735,960 shares, Security Ownership p.183), dual-listed NYSE+SIX with full sell-side coverage from day one, there was no informational or forced-seller inefficiency to harvest. Even an extraordinary post-spin behavioral signal — $67.6M of open-market insider buying, zero sells — could not force a re-rate against a decelerating cycle.] As of spin time the document itself flagged deceleration: Q1 2025 Adjusted EBITDA $214M vs $284M a year earlier (margin 10% vs 13%), net loss margin 4%, and $856M cash used in operations in the quarter (MD&A interim highlights). Dividend policy discretionary, Swiss withholding tax complicates US holders' yield (Dividend Policy, p.75; Swiss tax sections). FPI status permits Swiss-law governance carve-outs (Summary, p.6). No open-market purchases were observable within the 14-day fire window — the buying began 2025-08-08.
dossiers.bull_case: As of spin time, from the information statement alone: the largest NA-pure-play building-solutions company — $11.7B FY2024 revenue, $1.3B net income, $3.2B Adjusted EBITDA at 27% margin (Summary; MD&A) — with a stated commitment to an investment-grade credit rating and a conservative capital structure (Summary, 'Committed to driving shareholder value'). Pro forma leverage is modest for a spinco: ~$5.04B long-term debt against $3.18B 2024 Adj EBITDA (~1.6x), $12.5B pro forma equity, plus an undrawn $2.0B revolver (Capitalization, p.76-77). Greenblatt tell present: Jan Jenisch, Holcim's chairman and turnaround architect, moved to lead the spinco and shows 1,263,008 Company Shares/vested options post-spin (Security Ownership, p.183), and Cycle 2025/2026 PSUs were reset to Amrize standalone pro-forma goals (Treatment of Parent Equity Awards, p.60). Schmidheiny family anchor at 8.66% provides a stable long-term holder.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: Modest by design, which explains the shallow -10% dump. Holders received 1 AMRZ per Holcim share (Summary of the Separation, distribution ratio 1:1). Potential forced sellers: Swiss/European index trackers (Holcim is an SMI heavyweight) whose mandates exclude a US-primary name — but AMRZ dual-listed on NYSE AND SIX (Summary, p.1), letting most Swiss institutions keep it; UBS Fund Management (5.91%) and BlackRock (5.45%) are largely index money that could migrate rather than dump. Schmidheiny vehicles (8.66%, Security Ownership p.183-184) were sticky anchor capital. No US index inclusion at spin and no S&P 500 membership meant no offsetting forced BUYING either. Net: small structural sell pressure, quickly absorbed — and correspondingly no post-dump snapback edge.
dossiers.key_risk: As of spin time: cyclical deceleration already visible in the document (Q1 2025 Adj EBITDA -25% YoY, 10% margin vs 13%) in a US construction/housing downcycle, with zero neglect premium available — a $28B, fully-covered, dual-listed spin prices efficiently immediately, so gate arithmetic offers no re-rating edge to pay for the cycle risk.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Channel NOT dark despite FPI status — Amrize elected domestic-form reporting (10-K/10-Q/8-K per information statement Summary p.6-7) and files Section 16 Form 4s. Sweep of all 69 Form 4 filings since distribution (2025-06-23): 132 transactions, 83 open-market purchases (code P) by 10 insiders totaling $67,567,592, ZERO open-market sells. CEO Jan Jenisch alone bought ~$52M: ~1.06M shares across 2025-08-08/11 at $46.25-$47.99, 110,000 shares 2025-10-30 at ~$52.75, 60,000 shares 2026-03-06 at $58.05, and ~47,400 more in May 2026 at $48.50-$49.96. Broad C-suite participation: Johnston, Hill, Gross, Brouwer, Singleton, Gangestad, Forrest, Poletti, Clark all bought. Critically for window calibration: the first purchase came 2025-08-08 — 46 days after distribution and a month after the 2025-07-07 fire date — so this signal was unobservable at entry, and even at this magnitude it did not produce a re-rate within 12 months.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): gates scored AS OF SPIN TIME from the 10-12B/A information statement only; known outcome quarantined to prose. Capitalization as of 2025-03-31 (p.76-77): pro forma cash $40M (vs $574M historical — parent swept the cash), long-term debt $5,036M (vs $981M historical) via $3.4B Senior Unsecured Notes issued April 2025 plus ~$1.7B debt-for-debt exchange bonds via FinanceCo and $50M USPP assumption; related-party notes ($7.66B) extinguished. Total equity $12,468M pro forma; total capitalization $17,509M. $2.0B revolver undrawn pre-spin; $1.7B bridge expected unused/terminated. Against $3,181M FY2024 Adj EBITDA, gross leverage ~1.6x with a stated IG-rating commitment — a clean barge by spinco standards, hence garbage_barge_risk cut from 1.0 default to 0.4 (residual: cash-stripped to $40M, Q1 seasonal $856M operating cash outflow, Q1 EBITDA -25% YoY, and standard mutual tax/separation indemnities in Risk Factors). Incentive 0.45: document shows only converted parent awards (lowest evidence tier) plus mid-tier spinco-metric evidence — Cycle 2025/2026 PSUs reset to Amrize standalone pro-forma goals and a new 2025 Omnibus Plan denominated in Company Shares — and CEO Jenisch's 1.26M-share personal position; no observed open-market buys at spin, so the >=0.5 own-bar is not met from the document. Neglect 0.1: ~$28B market cap, dual NYSE/SIX listing, marquee parent. CALIBRATION LESSON: the gates worked — this scored watch, not own, and it duded exactly as a low-neglect big-cap should, even though the post-spin insider tell later fired at maximum strength.

dossiers.bear_case: Scored at spin time: the Form 10's own MD&A shows Test & Measurement in freefall — Q1'25 segment sales $188.5M vs $244.2M (-22.9% GAAP, -21.3% core), swinging to an $(11.9)M operating LOSS from +$57.7M, with Western Europe down high-fifties, driven by demand weakness across all end markets (MD&A, Comparison of Results, p.68 area). Pro forma Q1'25 net earnings of $51.0M vs $116.2M historical prior year. The entire ~$1.15B debt raise exits as a Cash Distribution to Fortive as partial consideration for contributed assets (Summary; Capitalization note 1) — spinco keeps the debt, parent keeps the cash. Incentive alignment is thin on paper: management ownership is trivial (Newcombe 65,024 shares, under 1%, all derived from converted Fortive holdings), the founder awards are one-time grants rather than performance-contingent, and outgoing Fortive PSUs convert into plain Ralliant RSUs with performance conditions collapsed to actual/target (Treatment of Outstanding Equity Awards, p.136) — a downgrade in pay-for-performance linkage. China is 15% of revenue ($322.7M FY2024, and $214.7M of it in the already-weak T&M segment), with tariff escalation explicitly flagged in Risk Factors. Dividend policy undetermined. [OUTCOME: known — the stock did fall another ~18% below the fire price before recovering; the bear case was 'right' for seven months before being wrong.]
dossiers.bull_case: As of spin, from the document alone: a real, profitable industrial — FY2024 revenue $2,154.7M, pro forma adjusted EBITDA $552.0M (25.6% margin), pro forma net earnings $303.0M — spun with only ~2.1x gross / ~1.8x net leverage and a $750M undrawn revolver. Sensors & Safety Systems ($1,217.2M revenue) is a stable utilities/aero-defense/industrial franchise offsetting the cyclical Test & Measurement side. New CEO Newcombe receives a $2,125,000 founder's equity award plus $1M incremental grant denominated in Ralliant stock (offer letter dated 2025-02-24, Executive Compensation section), and the standalone thesis (focused capital allocation, FBS-derived operating system) is coherent. The T&M downcycle disclosed in MD&A (Q1'25 oscilloscope/precision-instrument demand collapse) is exactly the kind of trough-at-spin condition that sets up a cyclical recovery under fresh coverage. [OUTCOME: known — spun 2025-06-30, dumped -21% in two weeks, Nemesis fast-tier fired 2025-07-14 at $47.36; price first fell further to the high-$30s (where six insiders bought $601,878 open-market in Feb 2026), then re-rated to $68.36 by 2026-07-03, +44% from the fire price. Graduated. Note the gates as scored here would have said WATCH, not OWN — the incentive gate could not clear 0.5 from the document alone, and the confirming insider buys arrived only in Aug 2025/Feb 2026, after the window.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: Parent Fortive (NYSE: FTV) is an S&P 500 large-cap; its register is dominated by large-cap index and quality/growth mandates. Distribution: 1 RAL for every 3 FTV shares, record date 2025-06-16, distributed 12:01 a.m. ET 2025-06-28 — 100% of ~112,990,276 shares hit the float at once, no lockups, no retained parent stake (Fortive Form 4 code J divesting 112,730,036 shares on 2025-06-28). Pre-spin 5% holders T. Rowe Price Investment Management (10.92%) and Vanguard (11.53%) inherit stakes mechanically (Security Ownership, p.146). Active FTV holders own the parent for the higher-multiple, ~50%-recurring-revenue IOS/AHS businesses (chairman's letter); a ~$5B mid-cap cyclical instruments company with a segment in operating loss does not fit those mandates — natural indiscriminate selling in the first 2-4 weeks. Index treatment of RAL post-spin is not disclosed in the Form 10. The observed -21% two-week dump matches this map.
dossiers.key_risk: Depth and duration of the Test & Measurement downcycle: the segment was already at an operating loss in the last quarter disclosed before the spin (Q1'25, -22.9% sales), and with no observable insider open-market buying at spin time there was no informed-money signal that the trough was in.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Channel is LIVE (US domestic issuer, not an FPI — Form 4s flow normally). At spin time and through the 2025-07-14 fire date: zero open-market purchases; only 2025-06-30 director initial grants (code A) including two fee-denominated acquisitions at $48.49 (Muller $100,035; Mitchell $60,031) and Fortive's code-J divestiture of 112.7M shares. Full sweep 2025-06-30 to 2026-07-03 (65 txns): 6 insiders made 7 open-market buys (code P) totaling $601,878 — first was Moorthy (Director) $90,340 @ $45.17 on 2025-08-13, then a cluster 2026-02-06/10 at $39.27-$42.48: Kazmi (CTO) $99,942, Worrell $99,825, Moorthy $79,480, Bryant $49,962, Sacks $82,500, Mitchell $99,828. Sells: Bick (CPO) $364,470 across Mar 2026; Osben (CAO) exercise-and-sell $1.69M @ $62.44 on 2026-05-28. Net read: a genuine multi-insider buy cluster formed seven months post-spin at prices ~17% BELOW the fast-tier fire price, then the stock re-rated to $68 — the insider signal confirmed the bottom, but arrived far too late to gate the spin-window entry.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): scored from the 10-12B/A information statement (Exhibit 99.1, filed 2025-05-28) as of spin time; outcome quarantined to prose. Capitalization as of 2025-03-28 pro forma: cash $150.0M; long-term debt $1,146.8M (net of issuance costs); total equity $2,849.8M; total capitalization $3,996.6M; 113.3M shares at $0.01 par (Capitalization, p.47). Financing: Senior Credit Facilities up to $2.05B — $600M 18-month term, $700M 3-year term, $750M 5-year revolver; ~$1.15B of proceeds paid to Fortive as the Cash Distribution (Summary; Description of Material Indebtedness). Pro forma FY2024: net earnings $303.0M, EBITDA $544.4M, adjusted EBITDA $552.0M, adjusted net earnings $366.9M; interest expense $66.3M (Summary Historical and Unaudited Pro Forma data, p.48). Implied gross leverage ~2.1x / net ~1.8x PF 2024 adj EBITDA — moderate, not a garbage barge, which is why garbage_barge_risk earns down to 0.5 despite the levered dividend; it goes no lower because Q1'25 PF net earnings were $51.0M vs $116.2M a year earlier with T&M at an operating loss. Revenue FY2024 $2,154.7M: T&M $937.5M, Sensors & Safety Systems $1,217.2M; geography US $1,101.4M / China $322.7M / other $730.6M; end markets incl. aero-defense-space $340.2M, industrial mfg $410.8M, semis $188.3M (F-24 disaggregation). Dividend policy undetermined at spin. Calibration lesson: gates produce WATCH from this document (incentive 0.35 < 0.5), yet the trade graduated — the fast-tier forced-seller mechanism, not the own-gates, carried this one.

dossiers.bear_case: The Form 10 predicted death in arithmetic, not adjectives. (1) Debt: $416.7M Twin Securitization Notes at a 9.50% weighted-average fixed rate (tranches at 9.00%/10.00%/11.00%), issued November 2024 to refinance the Prior Securitization Notes from FAT's leveraged 2021 Twin Peaks / 2023 Smokey Bones acquisitions — implied ~$48M/yr debt service against FY2023 Adjusted EBITDA of $28.3M and 39-week FY2024 Adjusted EBITDA of $16.6M. Debt service exceeded the company's best-ever cash generation before a dollar of capex. (2) Liquidity: pro forma cash $7,942K vs a first quarterly P&I payment of ~$12.1M due 2025-04-25 (Risk Factors) — the first coupon exceeded the entire cash balance ~90 days post-spin, with a DSCR covenant permitting acceleration. (3) Solvency: pro forma total stockholders' equity of $(51,628)K, accumulated deficit $(65,945)K, net losses in 4 of the last 5 fiscal years, net loss margin (13.5)% for the 39 weeks ended 2024-09-29. (4) Parent extraction/structure: FAT retained 94.2% of Class A plus 100% of 50-vote Class B = 98.6% voting power, with registration rights after 180 days to dump the stake — a spin designed to create a currency for an indicted parent (DOJ Sarbanes-Oxley 402 indictment and SEC complaint over Wiederhorn related-party loans, disclosed in Risk Factors), not to free the business. Noteholders extracted warrants on 2,364,913 shares just to place the notes. The garbage_barge gate scores 1.0 on this document; the reading would have said AVOID at spin time with no knowledge of the outcome. [OUTCOME: Chapter 11 on 2026-01-26 — twelve months post-spin, the securitization creditors own the estate; the gate worked.]
dossiers.bull_case: The spin-time bull (scored from the document only): Twin Peaks is a differentiated 'sports lodge' concept with a franchising flywheel — Summary section cites Adjusted EBITDA growth from $9.0M (FY2019) to $28.3M (FY2023), a 33.2% CAGR, Restaurant-Level Contribution Margin of 11.9-14.2%, and a franchised development pipeline; 172 system-wide restaurants after the Smokey Bones contribution. Extreme neglect was real: only ~5% of fully-diluted Class A distributed (0.1520207 per FAT share), no roadshow, no index eligibility (dual-class excluded from S&P/FTSE per the risk factors), so a forced-seller washout followed by rerating was the textbook Greenblatt setup IF the entity could survive its own capital structure. It could not, and the document said so. [OUTCOME: TWNP filed Chapter 11 jointly with parent FAT Brands and all its subsidiaries on 2026-01-26, In re Fat Brands Inc., Case 26-90126 (ARP), Bankr. S.D. Tex., per the 2026-03-30 8-K; Form 25-NSE delisting 2026-06-04; equity now ~$0.04 vs $0.83 when Nemesis fired 2026-01-08, i.e. -95%. This dossier is retrospective and entered no trades.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: (1) FAT Brands retail/insider holders receiving 0.1520207 TWNP per FAT share — sub-$500 odd lots with no reason to hold a sub-$100M-float restaurant spinco. (2) Index/ETF holders of FAT: TWNP joined no index, and the information statement itself flags S&P DJI/FTSE Russell dual-class exclusion rules — mechanical non-inclusion means the distributed 5% had no natural buyer. (3) The permanent overhang: FAT's retained 44,571,771 Class A + 2,870,000 Class B (94.2% economic) with registration rights exercisable 180 days post-spin — an indicted, over-levered parent that must monetize the stake, capping any rally (FAT in fact moved 7.1M TWNP shares in a June 2025 Form 4 related-party transaction). Note the inversion of the Greenblatt setup: here the forced-seller flow never cleared because the largest seller was the controlling parent itself.
dossiers.key_risk: As of spin time: debt service (~$48M/yr on $416.7M of 9.5% securitization notes, first $12.1M payment due 2025-04-25) structurally exceeds Adjusted EBITDA ($28.3M FY2023) with only $7.9M pro forma cash and a DSCR acceleration covenant — default was a timing question, not a probability question. Secondary: 98.6%-voting parent under DOJ indictment with registration rights to liquidate its 94.2% stake into the float.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 sweep since distribution (2025-01-29), via nemesis.insiders on CIK 0002011954: zero open-market purchases by any officer or director in the first weeks of trading — the one unengineerable conviction signal never fired. Whole period to date: one individual code-P buy, Kim Allen Boerema, 4,200 shares @ $3.70 (~$15.6K) on 2025-07-31, six months post-spin; parent FAT Brands filed a code-P for 7,139,667 shares @ $4.37 on 2025-06-04, which is a related-party/parent share movement, not a management cash buy (raw sweep sums $31.2M 'bought' but $31.2M of it is the parent line — excluded from judgment). Zero code-S sells. Pre-spin ownership table: all 8 NEOs/directors held 0 spinco shares before the spin and 46,025 as a group after, entirely via the FAT distribution ratio (e.g., FAT co-CEO Kenneth J. Kuick 16,722). Equity comp was a one-time 4,742,346-share Management Equity Plan of RSUs vesting 25%/6mo then annually on continued employment only — retention paper, no spinco performance metrics, no purchases. Channel effectively dark on management conviction; darkness confirmed the paper read.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): scored from the 2025-01-10 information statement (ex99-1 to Form 10-12B/A) as of spin time; known outcome excluded from all scores. Capitalization table (as of 2024-09-29, pro forma): cash $7,942K; current LTD $8,205K; LTD net of current $408,100K; due to affiliates $10,000K; total stockholders' equity (deficit) $(51,628)K; accumulated deficit $(65,945)K; total capitalization $364,677K. Debt = $416.7M Twin Securitization Notes (A-2-II $269.3M @9.00%, B-2 $57.6M @10.00%, M-2 $77.7M @11.00%; anticipated call 2027, legal final 2054), net proceeds ~$407.5M used to redeem the Prior Securitization Notes — i.e., the spinco was launched carrying the full refinanced cost of the parent's acquisition debt at a higher blended rate, plus Noteholders' Warrants on 2,364,913 shares. First quarterly P&I ~$12.1M due 2025-04-25 vs $7.9M cash. Pro forma 39-week FY2024: revenue $267.3M, net loss margin (13.5)%. Share count 47,298,271 Class A + 2,870,000 Class B (50 votes/sh); ~2.73M Class A actually floated (5% of fully diluted, incl. 2,659,490 per the parent's 2025-01-27 Form 4 line). Gate verdict from document alone: garbage_barge_risk 1.0 (nothing to subtract — negative equity, cash < one coupon, EBITDA < debt service), incentive_alignment 0.1 (time-vested retention RSUs only, zero insider skin), so AVOID regardless of neglect (0.9). This is the reference case proving the gate reads the right sections.

dossiers.bear_case: Scored at spin time, the incentive picture was paper-only. Security Ownership (pp.155-156): CEO David Sewell owned 0 Honeywell shares convertible to SOLS; all 15 directors and executive officers combined would hold 8,101 SOLS shares — economically zero on a 158.7M-share company. No insider had bought anything with personal money, and the Form 10 promises only grants: converted Honeywell RSUs/options plus time-based Founder's RSUs with no spinco performance metrics. Worse, converted PCUs "will have performance deemed achieved at the actual level of performance immediately prior to the Distribution Date... and will continue to vest solely based on service conditions" (Treatment of Outstanding Equity Awards, p.150) — legacy performance pay was converted to time-vesting on the way out the door. Honeywell extracted a ~$1.5B cash distribution funded by the $2.0B new debt (Summary, p.2), the textbook parent-enrichment pattern. The Separation Agreement contractually allocates to SOLS "all environmental liabilities" of the Advanced Materials business INCLUDING previously discontinued or divested operations primarily managed by the business, with SOLS indemnifying Honeywell (Risk Factors p.33; Separation Agreement—Liabilities, p.159), against only $53M accrued (Combined FS environmental note) — an open-ended fluorochemicals legacy tail. Dividend policy undecided with debt covenants limiting payments (p.46), guaranteeing income-mandate Honeywell holders would sell. [OUTCOME: none of these bear items bit within the ghost window; the stock roughly doubled off the dump. The env-tail and zero-skin concerns remain live for longer horizons but were irrelevant to the 1-4 month re-rating.]
dossiers.bull_case: As of the Form 10 (10-12B/A, 2025-09-30, Ex-99.1): a genuinely good business dressed as a spinoff, not a garbage barge. Leading global LGWP refrigerant franchise (Solstice/Genetron brands) plus electronic materials/Spectra fiber, 5,700+ patents, 3,000+ customers in ~120 countries (Summary, pp.1-5). 2024 pro forma: $3,770M net sales, $442M net income (~11.7% margin), pro forma interest of $139M covered ~5.4x by pre-interest pretax income (Unaudited Pro Forma Combined Statement of Operations, p.68). Net debt ~$1.52B ($1,971M debt less $450M cash) against that earnings power is modest for a forced-leverage spin. The Risk Factors explicitly telegraph the Nemesis setup: "It is likely that some Honeywell shareowners, including some of its larger shareowners, will sell their shares... in the case of index funds, we are not a participant in the index in which they are investing" (p.45-46) — the forced-seller dump was disclosed in advance. Fresh management got Founder's Grant RSUs ($5.0M CEO Sewell, $0.75-1.5M other NEOs) cliff-vesting 50%/50% at years 3 and 4 (Offer Letters table, p.153) — a real multi-year spinco equity horizon even without personal capital. [OUTCOME: spun 2025-10-28, dumped to ~$41.43 in the index-flow window, Nemesis ghost fired ~2025-11-13 @ $45.73, time-stopped ~+79%, $82.95 as of 2026-07-03. The balance-sheet/business-quality half of the read was decisively vindicated; the incentive gate, scored honestly, would have held this at watch — the calibration lesson is that on large-cap spins of quality assets, the forced-seller mechanics and clean pro forma can carry the trade even when insiders show zero personal skin at spin.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: Parent Honeywell (HON) is an S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average mega-cap; SOLS distributed 1-for-4 to HON holders of record 2025-10-17, ~158.7M shares out, ~$6.6B market value at the ~$41.43 trough. Mechanical sellers a reader could map from the document: (1) DJIA trackers — SOLS would not inherit Dow membership; (2) S&P 500 index funds if SOLS was not retained in the index (the Form 10 itself flags: larger shareowners "in the case of index funds" will sell if "we are not a participant in the index in which they are investing," Risk Factors pp.45-46); (3) pro forma 5% holders Vanguard 9.63% and BlackRock 6.66% (Security Ownership p.155) are predominantly passive vehicles subject to exactly that mechanic; (4) income/dividend mandates — HON pays a dividend, SOLS offered "no assurance that we will initiate the payment of a dividend" plus covenant limits (p.46); (5) mega-cap size mandates cutting a $6-7B mid-cap. Offsetting absorption: at ~$6.6B SOLS was large and liquid enough for rapid mid/large-cap index adds, arguing for a short flow window — consistent with the observed ~2-week dump and rebound.
dossiers.key_risk: Open-ended contractual assumption of ALL Advanced Materials environmental liabilities — including legacy sites of discontinued/divested operations and offsite waste disposal, with indemnification running TO Honeywell — against only a $53M accrual, in a chemicals/fluorochemicals business where "ultimate environmental costs and liabilities are difficult to predict and may significantly vary from current estimates" (Risk Factors p.33; Separation Agreement—Liabilities p.159). Notably the information statement contains zero mentions of PFAS, an omission a reader of a fluorochemical carve-out should register as unquantified rather than absent.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Pre-spin (Form 10, Security Ownership pp.155-156): CEO David Sewell 0 shares; NEO Jeffrey Dormo 0; all 15 directors and executive officers as a group only 8,101 expected SOLS shares (from 32,416 HON at 1-for-4) — effectively zero personal capital at stake at distribution. Compensation is all grants: converted HON RSUs/unvested options, converted PCUs with performance deemed achieved (service-vesting only), and time-based Founder's Grant RSUs (Sewell $5.0M; Pierce $1.5M; Rudick/Dormo/Mawson $1.0M; Clifford $0.75M) cliff-vesting 50% each at the 3rd and 4th anniversaries (pp.150-153). Post-spin Form 4 sweep (nemesis.insiders, CIK 0002064953, since 2025-10-28, run 2026-07-03): 29 Form 4 transactions filed, ZERO open-market purchases (code P), ZERO open-market sales (code S) — all activity is compensation mechanics (grants/conversions/withholding), including the 2026-03-12 director-grant batch and 2026-05/06 filings. SOLS is a domestic filer, not an FPI — the Form 4 channel was open, so the absence of code-P buys through the entire dump-to-$41 window is a genuine behavioral null: nobody with the internal numbers spent personal money at the lows, and nobody sold either.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): scored from the 10-12B/A Ex-99.1 information statement (filed 2025-09-30) as of spin time; the known outcome appears only in prose, never in scores. Capitalization (as of 6/30/25, p.65): pro forma cash $450M vs $873M historical; long-term debt $0 → $1,971M ($2.0B raised: senior secured term loan + 5.625% Senior Notes due 2033, weighted average rate ~5.8%); ~$1.5B of proceeds distributed to Honeywell ("Solstice Advanced Materials Cash Distribution," Summary p.2); total equity $3,473M historical → $1,189M pro forma; plus undrawn $1.0B revolver and $750M bilateral LC facilities. Pro forma P&L: FY2024 net sales $3,770M, pretax $610M, net income to SOLS $442M (~$2.79/sh on 158.7M shares); 1H2025 net sales $1,930M, net income $156M, EPS $0.98 (pp.67-68). Implied net debt ~$1.52B, roughly ~1.7x a reader's ~$900M EBITDA estimate — moderate, not barge-grade, despite the parent extraction. Balance-sheet blemishes: $164M accumulated OCI loss and negative $69M noncontrolling interest carried over; environmental accrual just $53M (FS notes) versus an unbounded contractual allocation of Advanced Materials environmental liabilities including divested-site legacy (p.159). Garbage gate at 0.5: real earnings, sane leverage, real cash — but parent-serving $1.5B dividend, all env liabilities assumed, and carve-out financials with unfinalized agreements keep it from scoring cleaner. Incentive gate at 0.4: no open-market buys existed or could exist pre-spin, D&O ownership de minimis (8,101 shares group-wide, p.156); credit earned only from spinco-denominated Founder's Grants with 3/4-year cliffs and annual LTI in SOLS equity (CEO ~$10.6M equity vs $1.06M salary, p.153) plus converted parent awards; debited for PCUs converted to service-only vesting with performance "deemed achieved" (p.150) and a 10,000,000-share Equity Plan (~6.3% dilution authorization). Under the law incentive 0.4 < 0.5 forbids "own" — the honest at-spin verdict was watch. That is the calibration finding: the gates as written would have sat out a ~+79% graduate; the document's own index-fund-selling disclosure plus a 0.5 garbage score was the tradeable half of the signal.

dossiers.bear_case: Classic debt-for-dividend mechanics: Qnity incurred $4.1B ($1.0B 5.750% Secured Notes 2032, $750M 6.250% Unsecured Notes 2033, $2.35B 7-yr term loan) to pay DuPont a Qnity Cash Distribution of ~$4.122B (Summary; Description of Material Indebtedness) — the parent extracted essentially all the debt capacity. Worse, the Separation Agreement contractually allocates Qnity the 'Applicable Qnity Percentage' (~44%, set by relative Pro Forma Operating EBITDA) of DuPont's legacy liabilities: Legacy Liabilities under the Corteva Letter Agreement, MOU funding obligations, legacy PFAS, and discontinued/divested businesses — with only $75M of indemnification liability recorded at separation against an open-ended tail (Risk Factors; Our Relationship with DuPont; NJ $875M settlement discussion). Incentive paper was weak: executives received converted DuPont RSUs/PSUs as a MIX of DuPont and Qnity RSUs (only partial spinco exposure; PSUs de-risked into RSUs at actual performance), no founders grant tied to Qnity metrics, and the expected D&O group owned 160,679 shares — under 0.1% of ~209.4M shares out (Treatment of Outstanding Equity Awards; Security Ownership).
dossiers.bull_case: A high-quality semis-materials franchise mispriced by holder-base mismatch: 2024 net sales $4,335M (+7% YoY), reportable segment Operating EBITDA $1,322M (Semiconductor Technologies $874M, Interconnect Solutions $448M, ICS +28% H1'25) — roughly 30% margins with data-center/advanced-packaging exposure (Business and MD&A sections). DuPont retains no stake; 100% distributed 1-for-2 to DD holders, so a dividend-industrial register receives a levered growth cyclical it never chose, and the Form 10's own dividend policy (~10% of net income) guarantees income holders exit. Pro forma balance sheet is serviceable, not distressed: $661M cash, $4,025M borrowings vs ~$1.3B EBITDA (Capitalization, p.77). [OUTCOME: this is retrospective — Q spun 2025-11-01, dumped -24% to $73.54 by Thanksgiving, Nemesis fired 2025-12-02 @ $79.75, time-stopped ~+80%, now $153.37. Graduated. The bull case above is what the document supported at spin; the outcome is context, not an input to the gate scores.]
dossiers.forced_seller_map: DD's register at spin was index- and income-heavy: Vanguard 11.5% and BlackRock 7.3% of expected Q shares (Security Ownership, from DD 13Gs). Index flow was largely retained (Q, ~209M shares, mega-cap, NYSE-listed, S&P-eligible), so the true forced sellers were style-mismatched active holders: (1) dividend/income funds — DD pays a full dividend while Q's stated policy is only ~10% of net income (Dividend Policy, p.76); (2) industrial/chemicals-mandate funds that cannot hold a levered semiconductor-materials cyclical; (3) risk-off holders spooked by the 44% PFAS allocation headline in Risk Factors. The -24% slide to $73.54 by Thanksgiving matches that map: a several-week active-holder purge in a name too large for true index orphaning, hence a fast (weeks, not quarters) clearing — consistent with the 2025-12-02 fire and subsequent recovery.
dossiers.key_risk: Open-ended ~44% allocation of DuPont legacy PFAS/discontinued-operations liabilities via the Separation Agreement (only $75M recorded at separation; NJ settlement alone is $875M over 25 years at the parent level), stacked on $4.0B of fresh spin-related debt — a liability tail that scales with future PFAS outcomes entirely outside the electronics business's control.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Domestic filer — Form 4 channel live (not an FPI). Sweep since distribution (2025-11-01): 76 transactions; 2 open-market purchases totaling $329,572 by 2 outside directors — Byron Green 1,000 sh @ $79.12 (2025-11-19) and Anne Noonan 3,240 sh @ $77.30 (2025-11-21) — both INSIDE the forced-seller dump window and before the 2025-12-02 fire; 5 insiders sold $970,297 across 6 transactions, including NEO Sang Ho Kang selling 2,500 sh @ $74.50 into the dump (2025-11-21), CEO Jon Kemp 5,655 sh @ $85.01 and Michael Goss 1,895 sh @ $84.79 (2025-12-11), and director Steven Sterin 911 sh @ $148-162 (May-Jun 2026). At fire time the visible tape was two director buys (~$330K) against one officer sell — a modest behavioral confirm that would have justified nudging incentive_alignment up from the paper-only 0.2, though officer (vs director) money never showed up on the buy side.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: RETROSPECTIVE FAST-TIER READ (operator-directed 2026-07-03): scores set from the 10-12B/A information statement as of spin time; outcome excluded from gates. Capitalization as of 6/30/25 pro forma (p.77): cash $661M; borrowings $4,025M ($24M ST, $4,001M LT); total equity $7,405M (APIC $7,338M, NCI $268M); total capitalization $11,430M. Debt = $1.0B 5.750% Secured Notes '32 + $750M 6.250% Unsecured Notes '33 (both issued 8/15/25) + $2.35B 7-yr term loan drawn at spin; $1.25B undrawn 5-yr revolver. Proceeds + cash on hand funded the ~$4.122B Qnity Cash Distribution to DuPont (incl. $22M notes costs and $66M pre-funded interest). Leverage vs 2024 reportable segment Operating EBITDA of $1,322M (less $25M corporate): ~3.1x gross, ~2.6x net — levered but serviceable for a 30%-margin grower. Contingents: Separation Agreement allocates the Applicable Qnity Percentage (illustratively 44% by relative Pro Forma Operating EBITDA) of Legacy Liabilities, MOU Qualified Spend (DuPont had borne ~$645M of ~$1.4B cap, $217M accrued at parent), legacy PFAS and discontinued/divested-business liabilities; Qnity expected to record $75M indemnification liability at separation, excluding its share of the NJ $875M/25-yr settlement. Gate verdict at spin: incentive 0.2 (converted parent awards only, mixed DD/Q RSU treatment, D&O <0.1%) fails the >=0.5 own-gate; garbage 0.6 is borderline. Calibration lesson: the incentive gate produced a false negative on a name that graduated +80% — the garbage gate correctly read the balance sheet as non-fatal, and the forced-seller map, not the incentive paper, carried this trade.
